title: potentilla norvegica f. degenerata
intro: Potentilla norvegica f. degenerata, also known as the Norwegian cinquefoil or five-finger grass, is a flowering plant in the rose family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and as an anti-inflammatory agent. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, contributing to its potential health benefits, including improving cardiovascular health and reducing inflammation. Potentilla norvegica f. degenerata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional folk medicine, particularly in European and Asian cultures.
